title: Mespilus loddigesiana
intro: Mespilus loddigesiana, also known as Chinese rowan, is a deciduous shrub native to China, characterized by its showy white flowers and red berries. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate conditions such as arthritis and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Mespilus loddigesiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often used to prepare teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being dried and used as a medicinal powder.
taxonomy: Mespilus loddigesiana is classified within the family Rosaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the rose family. This family includes a wide range of species, from deciduous trees and shrubs to herbaceous perennials. The genus Mespilus is a small group of deciduous shrubs and small trees native to the northern hemisphere. Mespilus loddigesiana, specifically, is a type of flowering shrub native to China. It is also known as the Chinese hawthorn and is a popular ornamental plant in gardens.

constituents: Mespilus loddigesiana, also known as the Chinese hawthorn, contains various active constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoid saponins.
